claim: Post-synthesis view: BTC is still modestly more likely than not to resolve Yes, but the case is a narrow threshold-maintenance bet rather than a broad bullish BTC call. I place the Apr 17 noon ET Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute close above 74,000 at 0.58 to 0.62, essentially near market with only a very small bullish lean because direct verification confirms the governing mechanics and current above-strike regime, while fresh checks also reinforce that the cushion is thin enough for ordinary volatility to erase it before the exact settlement minute.

frontmatter.contract_ambiguity_reason: Final authority is Binance UI 1m candle close while most checks used strong API proxies rather than the literal settlement screen.
independently_verified_points: Polymarket rules explicitly require Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1m candle final Close strictly above 74,000
Current Binance BTCUSDT spot remained around 74.7k at synthesis time
Binance 24h range still straddled 74k, confirming the strike sits inside normal recent volatility
Polymarket visible 74k market was around mid-60s, directionally close to assignment baseline
frontmatter.verification_gap_summary: The main unverified point is how much current above-strike spot should translate into the exact Apr 17 noon ET close probability.
frontmatter.best_countercase_summary: A routine sub-1% to ~1% dip into the exact noon minute can still produce No even if BTC stays broadly firm.
frontmatter.main_reason_for_disagreement: Different weighting of narrow one-minute timing risk versus current above-strike regime.
frontmatter.resolution_mechanics_summary: Yes requires the Binance BTC/USDT Apr 17 12:00 ET 1-minute candle final Close to be strictly greater than 74,000.
frontmatter.freshness_driver: BTC intraday price action into the Apr 17 late-morning ET settlement window.
decision_blockers: Thin cushion above strike relative to normal BTC short-horizon volatility
Final settlement uses one exact Binance minute close rather than broader daily direction
Literal settlement authority is the Binance UI candle surface, not just API proxies
frontmatter.next_checkpoint: Recheck Binance BTC/USDT during the late morning ET approach to Apr 17 12:00 ET.

sections.Alpha summary: Market baseline was 0.605. My synthesized range is 0.58 to 0.62. That is marginal-to-unclear edge territory, not a clean actionable dislocation. The only plausible mispricing is that market participants may slightly over- or underweight exact-minute timing fragility relative to current above-74k spot, but fresh verification did not support a large deviation from market.
sections.Input coverage: All five personas were available and usable: base-rate, catalyst-hunter, market-implied, risk-manager, and variant-view. No persona was missing. I critically compared sidecars against raw findings; the sidecars looked broadly faithful, with variant-view the most compressed and somewhat more speculative because it leaned on an unbuilt 'intraday timing risk' framing without stronger independent quantification. Supporting evidence/artifact use was limited because the raw findings already contained the key provenance and contract details. Coverage is complete because all planned personas were present and no major evidentiary hole prevented synthesis.
sections.Market-implied baseline: The assigned market-implied probability was 0.605 at the bundle snapshot. Fresh synthesis-stage check of the Polymarket event page showed the visible 74k line around 66-67%, directionally close enough to treat the market as broadly stable rather than materially moved. The right baseline is therefore still 'market slightly favors Yes, but not decisively.'
sections.Syndicated probability estimate: My final post-synthesis estimate is 0.58 to 0.62 for Yes. This is my own range, not a mechanical median. It preserves the swarm's modest-Yes center while narrowing around the view that the true answer is probably close to market because the bullish fact pattern is real but fragile.
sections.Difference from swarm-implied center: The provisional swarm center was about 0.62. I moved slightly lower and tightened toward 0.60 because the synthesis-stage verification confirmed the strongest bullish premise already known to the swarm—BTC is above strike on Binance—but did not add new independent support strong enough to justify staying confidently above market. Fresh checks instead reinforced the risk-manager and variant-view caution that a thin cushion can easily be lost by the exact settlement minute.
sections.Agreement or disagreement with market: I roughly agree with market. My range straddles the market baseline, with only a slight lean that Yes remains the base case because spot is already above strike and no fresh breakout is needed. But I do not see a robust independently verified edge versus market.
sections.Independent verification of edge: Verification quality is medium. I independently rechecked Polymarket rules and confirmed the exact resolution mechanics: Binance BTC/USDT, 12:00 ET, 1-minute candle, final Close, strictly above 74,000. I also checked fresh Binance spot, 5-minute average, and 24h range, which confirmed BTC remained in the mid-74k regime and that 74k remained inside the recent realized range. I also checked the Polymarket event page and found the visible 74k price in the mid-60s. What remains weak is translation from current above-strike spot to the precise noon-close probability; no fresh conditional-distribution study or stronger intraday volatility model was added at synthesis.
sections.Compression toward market due to verific: Yes. The swarm's mild bullish lean was credible, but the extra truth-finding pass did not independently verify a meaningful positive edge over market. Because the gap versus market was small to begin with, the right compression was modest rather than dramatic: pull the final range toward market and avoid overstating a thin edge that depends on one exact minute.
sections.Timing and catalyst posture: The key catalyst is not a scheduled macro event identified in the bundle; it is simply price maintenance into the Apr 17 late-morning ET window. The edge, if any, is more likely to decay than widen if BTC drifts sideways near the threshold, because the market will keep updating on exact-minute fragility. Waiting closer to settlement would improve accuracy, but this also means the current view has high staleness risk.
sections.Decision blockers: There are no major contract blockers, but there are practical blockers to high conviction: the strike is close to spot, the contract is one-minute specific, and the final authoritative surface is the Binance UI candle rather than generic spot prints. Those are reasons for caution rather than reasons the case cannot be judged.
sections.Implication for the question: The best current synthesis is that Yes is still a modest favorite, but only barely. Operationally this should be treated as a narrow threshold-defense market where broad BTC bullishness is not enough; the exact noon ET Binance close controls.
sections.Consensus across personas: Broad consensus was strong on five points: the contract mechanics are clear; Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute Close is the governing source; BTC was already above 74k during analysis; no fresh upside breakout was required for Yes; and the thin cushion above strike makes this far from a lock. All personas also converged on the idea that this is primarily a timing-sensitive threshold market, not a broad fundamentals question.
sections.Key disagreements across personas: The main disagreement was timing/weighting-based. Base-rate, catalyst-hunter, and market-implied put more weight on current above-strike regime persistence and landed around 0.62-0.63. Risk-manager and variant-view put more weight on one-minute settlement fragility and landed at 0.57 and 0.55. There was also a minor interpretation/provenance disagreement about how much confidence to place in Binance API checks versus the literal Binance UI candle named in the rule, but no persona argued that this ambiguity was likely outcome-changing.
sections.Best countercase: The best countercase, best represented by risk-manager and variant-view, is that the market still slightly overstates Yes because a roughly 1% cushion is small for BTC and the contract can fail on a routine fade at exactly the wrong minute even if the broader tape stays constructive.
sections.Encapsulated assumptions: Shared assumptions: Binance remains operational and representative; current above-74k Binance trading is somewhat informative; no major shock radically changes regime before noon ET. Contested assumptions: how informative current spot is for the exact next-day noon close; whether market already fully prices one-minute timing fragility. Fragile assumptions: that Binance API/context checks will closely match the final UI settlement candle and that no sudden morning volatility event emerges.
sections.Encapsulated evidence map: Strongest supporting evidence: Binance spot and near-term averages remained above 74k; recent Binance closes and broader cross-venue context kept BTC in the mid-74k regime; market strike ladder looked internally coherent. Strongest contradictory evidence: Binance 24h range of roughly 73.5k to 75.4k straddled 74k; prior noon checkpoint cited by catalyst-hunter was below strike; only a modest dip is needed to flip the result. Governing evidence: Polymarket rules are explicit on exchange, pair, minute, close field, and strict inequality. Mixed evidence: API and contextual checks are strong, but not the literal settlement screen.
sections.Evidence weighting: I gave the most weight to explicit contract rules and same-venue Binance price context because this is a settlement-mechanics contract. I downweighted broad narrative reasoning and unsupported macro speculation because no decisive catalyst was identified. I also downweighted any attempt to infer a large edge from current spot alone, since the strike sits inside ordinary recent volatility.
sections.Counterpoints / strongest disconfirming: The strongest disconfirming evidence against a bullish lean is simply that BTC only needs a fairly ordinary downside move into the exact settlement minute to land below 74,000, and fresh Binance 24h data confirms that such a move is well within normal realized range.
sections.Resolution or source-of-truth interpreta: The source-of-truth interpretation is straightforward and mostly uncontested: Yes requires the Binance BTC/USDT candle for Apr 17 at 12:00 ET, on 1-minute setting, to have a final Close strictly above 74,000. Equality does not count. Other exchanges, other pairs, nearby timestamps, highs, or averages do not count. The only minor nuance is that research largely used Binance API/context while Polymarket names the Binance trading interface candle as the final authority.
sections.Why this could create or destroy alpha: If the market were mentally pricing a generic 'BTC still above 74k tomorrow' story instead of a one-minute Binance close, No could be underpriced. If instead market participants are overemphasizing timestamp fragility and underweighting current above-strike regime persistence, Yes could be slightly underpriced. The synthesis found no strong proof of either error, which is why the likely alpha is small and fragile.
sections.What would falsify this interpretation /: A sustained move materially above about 75.5k into the Apr 17 morning would push the view higher because the cushion would become meaningfully safer. A clean loss of 74k, or even sustained trade in the low-74k/high-73k area into late morning ET, would push the view lower quickly. Any evidence of Binance-specific settlement-surface irregularity would also change the view.
sections.Highest-value next research: The single highest-value next check is a direct Binance late-morning ET read on Apr 17 focused on whether BTC still holds a meaningful cushion above 74,000 and whether minute-level volatility is compressing or expanding into noon.
sections.Source-quality assessment: Primary source class relied on most: Polymarket rules plus Binance same-venue market data. Most important secondary source class: cross-venue spot/context checks and the Polymarket strike ladder. Evidence independence is medium: several sources are distinct but still all tied to the same narrow price-observation problem. Source-of-truth ambiguity is low to minor, not zero, because the final authority is a Binance UI candle while most runtime checks use APIs. The synthesis is not bottlenecked by missing personas, but it is bottlenecked by the natural limits of forecasting one exact future minute close without a richer conditional-volatility study.
sections.Verification impact: Yes, additional synthesis-stage verification was used. It materially helped on contract mechanics and current same-venue price context, but it did not materially uncover a hidden edge. Cross-lane comparison also made clear that risk-manager and variant-view were not disagreeing on facts so much as on how heavily to discount current spot for exact-minute fragility. I did not find major raw-vs-sidecar distortions beyond variant-view being somewhat more compressed and hypothesis-forward.
sections.Persona contribution map: base-rate — supplied the clean outside-view argument that current above-strike regime should make Yes modestly favored, but not by much. catalyst-hunter — best framed the remaining catalyst as simple threshold maintenance into the exact minute and added the useful prior-day noon close below strike. market-implied — best tested whether the market itself already looked broadly efficient and concluded it mostly did. risk-manager — most clearly articulated why one-minute, exchange-specific settlement mechanics should compress confidence and limit edge claims. variant-view — preserved the strongest below-market countercase that intraday timing/path dependence may still be slightly underweighted.
sections.Reusable lesson signals: Possible durable lesson: narrow exchange-settled crypto threshold markets should be treated as settlement-surface contracts first and directional asset bets second. Possible underbuilt driver: an explicit intraday timing/path-dependence driver may be useful if similar markets recur. Possible source-quality lesson: API checks should be labeled as strong proxies when a UI candle is the formal source of truth. Reusability confidence: medium.
sections.Orchestrator review suggestions: Review later for durable lesson: yes. Review later for driver candidate: yes. Review later for canon or linkage issue: no. Review later for swarm-method issue: no. Reason: this case repeatedly surfaced one-minute settlement/path-dependence as a meaningful but not yet fully formalized driver family for narrow crypto threshold markets.
sections.Recommended follow-up: Wait for the Apr 17 late-morning checkpoint and refresh with a direct Binance-focused settlement-surface read. No rerun of the full swarm is needed unless price regime or venue-specific conditions change materially.
